description: Master structural map of the cross‑jurisdictional compliance and interoperability architecture for Beast System 3.0. Defines federal, state, county, municipal, and cross‑agency integration logic.

federal.purpose: Ensures alignment with federal constitutional standards, civil rights protections, and federal filing requirements.

state.purpose: Ensures compatibility with state‑level administrative, civil, and procedural standards.

county.purpose: Ensures interoperability with county‑level service systems, courts, and administrative bodies.

municipal.purpose: Ensures compatibility with city‑level systems, including housing, policing, and community services.

health_and_wellbeing.purpose: Ensures alignment with trauma‑informed, dignity‑first wellbeing standards.

housing_services.purpose: Ensures compatibility with housing stability, shelter systems, and non‑exploitative housing pathways.

public_safety.purpose: Ensures non‑punitive, harm‑reduction‑aligned interaction with public safety systems.

courts_and_legal.purpose: Ensures filings, exhibits, and governance documents meet judicial standards.

compliance_router.purpose: Routes compliance checks across jurisdictional layers.
rules: no downward override may violate dignity invariants

integration_engine.purpose: Ensures data, identity, and registry objects synchronize across systems.

conflict_resolution_engine.purpose: Resolves conflicts between jurisdictional rules using constitutional‑predictive logic.

rules: all compliance checks must be logged
no log may be deleted or altered

federal_context.author: Pat Tarwater Jr.
federal_context.notes: Compliance & Interoperability Layer is federally recognized and must remain immutable.
